zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Anne Coursey Madison於1516年出生在英國素瑞時，她的父親Sidney Madison36歲，母親Mary Ann McMinn16歲。她於1539年與Tristram Thomas在英國肯特設維寧結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1624年3月28日在英國英格蘭肯特坎特布瑞坎特布瑞 聖 瑪麗 馬格達倫享年108歲，埋葬在英國素瑞貝爾蒙。

nameMeanings.name: Madison
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of   Mattison   or   Mattinson   (‘son of Matthew’) with voicing of /t/ to /d/.
from the Middle English personal names Madde and Maddy + son; these may be pet forms of Maud (see Maude 2) or possibly voiced variants of Matte and Matty, pet forms of Matthew . Compare Madson .

variants.name: Mattison
meanings: English (northern): patronymic from the personal name Mattie (see at Mattey ) + -son. Compare Mathieson , Matson , Matthewson and Maddison .

Americanized form (and a very rare Swedish variant) of Swedish Mattisson: patronymic from the personal name Mattis, an equivalent of Matthew . This is also an Americanized form of some Danish, Norwegian, and North German cognates, most commonly Mathiesen and Matthiesen , and possibly also of the very rare Danish Mattissen. Compare Matteson .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

nameMeanings.name: Coursey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (Cork): shortened form of   Decoursey  .

variants.name: Decoursey
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, a variant of De Courcy, a habitational name for someone from any of various places in northern France called Courcy, especially the one in Calvados. Courcy is from the Gallo-Roman personal name Curtius (a derivative of Latin curtus ‘short’; compare Court 3) + the locative suffix -acum.
History: Patrick de Courcy was appointed justiciar of Ireland in 1221.

variants.name: Corsey
meanings: Irish: Cor, a knot, and lać, people, m eaning united people

variants.name: Cosey
meanings: Originally a North American name bestowed in honour of the American engine driver and folk hero ‘Casey’ Jones ( 1863–1900 ), who saved the lives of passengers on the ‘Cannonball Express’ at the expense of his own. He was baptized Johnathan Luther Jones in Cayce, Kentucky, and acquired his nickname from his birthplace. As a girl's name it is a variant of Cassie . In recent decades it has become equally popular in Britain, particularly as a name for girls. In part, this may reflect a transferred use of the Irish surname, a reduced Anglicized form of Ó Cahasaigh ‘descendant of Cathasach’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Anne Coursey Madison was born in 1516, in Surrey, England, her father, Sidney Madison, was 36 and her mother, Mary Ann McMinn, was 16. She married Tristram Thomas in 1539, in Chevening, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 28 March 1624, in Canterbury St Mary Magdalen, Canterbury, Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 108, and was buried in Bermondsey, Surrey, England.

ko.St Mary Magdalen, Bermondsey, Surrey, En: 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 캉르버이 캉르버이 스트 메리 막그다렌
ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Anne Coursey Madison(이)가 1516년 영국 수리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sidney Madison(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Ann McMinn(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 1539년 영국 켄트 처베닝에서 Tristram Thomas(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1624년 3월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 캉르버이 캉르버이 스트 메리 막그다렌에서 108세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수리 버몽시에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Anne Coursey Madison родилась в 1516 г. в местности сурри, Англия, её отцу по имени Sidney Madison было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Ann McMinn, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Tristram Thomas в 1539 г. в местности чевенинг, кэнт, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 28 марта 1624 г. в местности кантирбури ст мэри магдеилен, кантирбури, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 108 лет и была похоронена в местности бирмондси, сурри, Англия.
